title: Viewport Object
description: A viewport within Fusion. A viewport is the window where the model is displayed.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: fit
methods.description: Forces a camera change so that all of the graphics are visible in the viewport.

methods.name: goHome
methods.description: Sets the camera of the viewport to the defined "home" position.

methods.name: modelToViewSpace
methods.description: A specified point in model space returns the equivalent point in view space.

methods.name: refresh
methods.description: Forces the view to refresh. It is sometimes useful to force a refresh to be able to see edits that have been made using the API.

methods.name: resetFront
methods.description: Resets the front view to be the default front view orientation.

methods.name: saveAsImageFile
methods.description: Saves the current view to the specified image file. The view is re-rendered to the specified size and not just scaled from the existing view. This allows you to generate higher resolution images than you could do with just a screen capture.

methods.name: saveAsImageFileWithOptions
methods.description: Saves the current view to the specified image file. The view is re-rendered to the specified size and not just scaled from the existing view. This allows you to generate higher resolution images than you could do with just a screen capture.

methods.name: screenToView
methods.description: Converts a 2D screen point into the equivalent viewport coordinate.

methods.name: setCurrentAsFront
methods.description: Sets the "front" view to be the current view orientation.

methods.name: setCurrentAsHome
methods.description: Sets the "home" view to be the current view orientation.

methods.name: setCurrentAsTop
methods.description: Sets the "top" view to be the current view orientation.

methods.name: viewToModelSpace
methods.description: A specified point in view space returns the equivalent point in model space. Because view space is 2D and model space is 3D, the depth of the point is returned is somewhat arbitrary along the eye to target point direction.

methods.name: viewToScreen
methods.description: Converts a 2D viewPort point into the equivalent screen coordinate.

properties.name: camera
properties.description: Gets and sets the camera associated with the view. The camera returned is a copy of the current camera settings of the view. Editing the properties of the camera will have no affect on the viewport until the camera is assigned back to the viewport.

properties.name: frontEyeDirection
properties.description: Returns the direction of the front view as defined by the view cube. This vector defines the direction from the eye to the target for the front view.

properties.name: frontUpDirection
properties.description: Returns the up direction of the front view as defined by the view cube.

properties.name: height
properties.description: Returns the height of the viewport in pixels.

properties.name: isFullScreen
properties.description: Gets and sets if the view is in full screen mode.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: modelToViewSpaceTransform
properties.description: Returns a transformation matrix that defines the transform from model to viewport space.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: parentDocument
properties.description: Returns the parent document of this viewport.

properties.name: visualStyle
properties.description: Gets and sets the current visual style being used.

properties.name: width
properties.description: Returns the width of the viewport in pixels.

related_samples.name: As-Built Joint Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new As-Built Joint.

related_samples.name: Create Animation API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a series of images of a design where a parameter is being changed. The series of images can be used to create an animation using other software. To run this sample, have a part open that contains a parameter named "Length". The parameter should be able to be successfully modified from 10 to 15 centimeters. Run the sample and choose or create a directory for the output. After running you should have a folder full of images that are snapshots of each parameter value.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin.

related_samples.name: Rigid Group API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Rigid Group.

camera.title: Viewport.camera Property
camera.description: Gets and sets the camera associated with the view. The camera returned is a copy of the current camera settings of the view. Editing the properties of the camera will have no affect on the viewport until the camera is assigned back to the viewport.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = viewport_var.camera

# Set the value of the property.
viewport_var.camera = propertyValue

related_samples.name: As-Built Joint Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new As-Built Joint.

related_samples.name: Joint Origin Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Joint Origin.

related_samples.name: Rendering Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates using the Rendering capabilities in the API. This starts a series of local renderings to generate a series of frames, where the camera is repositioned and a parameter is modified for each frame to create a dynamic animation. To use the sample, have a design open that contains a parameter named "Length". It can be a user or model parameter. The sample will modify this parameter from a value of 0.1 cm to 15 cm, but you can change these values by editing the values of the paramStartVal and paramEndVal variables on lines 90 and 91 of the sample. It expects a folder named "C:\Temp\RenderSample" to exist, and will fail if it doesn't. The rendered frames will be written to that folder.An example rendering is shown below where this file was used. The parameter is modifying a move feature which results in changing the shape of an extrusion. The parameter could be driving anything and you could modify the code to edit more than one parameter. The result of this sample is a folder containing all of the rendered frames. You can process these to create an animation. The sample animation was created using GIMP.

related_samples.name: Rigid Group API Sample
related_samples.description: Demonstrates creating a new Rigid Group.

classType.title: Viewport.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

fit.title: Viewport.fit Method
fit.description: Forces a camera change so that all of the graphics are visible in the viewport.
fit.return_description: Returns true if the fit was successful.

goHome.title: Viewport.goHome Method
goHome.description: Sets the camera of the viewport to the defined "home" position.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = viewport_var.goHome()

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = viewport_var.goHome(transition)

goHome.return_description: Returns true if setting the view orientation was successful.

parameters.name: transition
parameters.description: If this is true it will do a smooth transition from the current camera position to the home position. If false, the view will jump to the home position with no intermediate steps.   This is an optional argument whose default value is True.

modelToViewSpace.title: Viewport.modelToViewSpace Method
modelToViewSpace.description: A specified point in model space returns the equivalent point in view space.
modelToViewSpace.return_description: Returns the equivalent point in view space.

refresh.title: Viewport.refresh Method
refresh.description: Forces the view to refresh. It is sometimes useful to force a refresh to be able to see edits that have been made using the API.
refresh.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

resetFront.title: Viewport.resetFront Method
resetFront.description: Resets the front view to be the default front view orientation.
resetFront.return_description: Returns true if resetting to front was successful.

saveAsImageFile.title: Viewport.saveAsImageFile Method
saveAsImageFile.description: Saves the current view to the specified image file. The view is re-rendered to the specified size and not just scaled from the existing view. This allows you to generate higher resolution images than you could do with just a screen capture.
saveAsImageFile.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: filename
parameters.description: The full filename, including the path, of the image file. The type of image file to be created is inferred from the extension of the filename.

parameters.name: width
parameters.description: The width in pixels of the output image. A value of zero indicates that the current width of the viewport is to be used.

parameters.name: height
parameters.description: The height in pixels of the output image. A value of zero indicates that the current height of the viewport is to be used.

related_samples.name: Create Animation API Sample
related_samples.description: Creates a series of images of a design where a parameter is being changed. The series of images can be used to create an animation using other software. To run this sample, have a part open that contains a parameter named "Length". The parameter should be able to be successfully modified from 10 to 15 centimeters. Run the sample and choose or create a directory for the output. After running you should have a folder full of images that are snapshots of each parameter value.

saveAsImageFileWithOptions.title: Viewport.saveAsImageFileWithOptions Method
saveAsImageFileWithOptions.description: Saves the current view to the specified image file. The view is re-rendered to the specified size and not just scaled from the existing view. This allows you to generate higher resolution images than you could do with just a screen capture.
saveAsImageFileWithOptions.return_description: Returns true if the operation was successful.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: A SaveImageFileOptions object that defines the various options that define how the image should be created. The SaveImageFileOptions can be created by using the static create method on the SaveImageFileOptions class.

screenToView.title: Viewport.screenToView Method
screenToView.description: Converts a 2D screen point into the equivalent viewport coordinate.
screenToView.return_description: Returns the equivalent point in the viewport. This can return null in the case where the input screen point does not lie within the viewport.

parameters.name: screenCoordinate
parameters.description: A 2D coordinate in screen space. (0,0) indicates the upper-left corner of the entire screen.

setCurrentAsFront.title: Viewport.setCurrentAsFront Method
setCurrentAsFront.description: Sets the "front" view to be the current view orientation.
setCurrentAsFront.return_description: Returns true if setting the view orientation was successful.

setCurrentAsHome.title: Viewport.setCurrentAsHome Method
setCurrentAsHome.description: Sets the "home" view to be the current view orientation.
setCurrentAsHome.return_description: Returns true if setting the view orientation was successful.

parameters.name: isFitToView
parameters.description: Specifies if when the view goes "home" if the view should be fit to the model or not. True indicates the view will be fit to the model.

setCurrentAsTop.title: Viewport.setCurrentAsTop Method
setCurrentAsTop.description: Sets the "top" view to be the current view orientation.
setCurrentAsTop.return_description: Returns true if setting the view orientation was successful.

viewToModelSpace.title: Viewport.viewToModelSpace Method
viewToModelSpace.description: A specified point in view space returns the equivalent point in model space. Because view space is 2D and model space is 3D, the depth of the point is returned is somewhat arbitrary along the eye to target point direction.
viewToModelSpace.return_description: Returns the equivalent point in model space.

viewToScreen.title: Viewport.viewToScreen Method
viewToScreen.description: Converts a 2D viewPort point into the equivalent screen coordinate.
viewToScreen.return_description: Returns the equivalent point in the screen. This can return null in the case where the input point is outside the bounds of the screen, which also means it's outside any viewport.

parameters.name: viewCoordinate
parameters.description: A 2D coordinate in the viewport. (0,0) indicates the upper-left corner of the viewport.
